title: linanthus floribundus subsp. hallii
intro: Linanthus floribundus subsp. hallii, also known as Hall's Linanthus or Hall's Purple Coyote Mint, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the Phlox family, characterized by its delicate purple flowers and minty fragrance. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antispasmodic properties, making it a potential remedy for various health issues, including digestive problems, respiratory issues, and wounds. The health benefits of Linanthus floribundus subsp. hallii include reducing inflammation, relieving pain, and promoting wound healing, as well as its potential to act as an antimicrobial agent to combat infections. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, tinctures, and salves, which can be used to soothe digestive issues, ease respiratory problems, and promote overall well-being.
